title: CLAUDE.md
text: making feature issue, follow following template:
code: ## Feature Request  ### Description [Provide a clear and concise description of the feature you are requesting. What problem does it aim to solve?]  ### Proposed Solution [Outline a proposed solution or approach for implementing the feature. How do you envision the feature working?]  ### Use Case [Describe a specific use case or scenario where this feature would be beneficial or necessary.]  ### Additional Context [Add any additional context, information, or examples that support or clarify the feature request.]  ### Impact [Explain the potential impact of this feature on the package and its users. How would it improve the package or benefit the user community?]  ### Related Issues [Are there any related issues or pull requests that are relevant to this feature request?]

title: CLAUDE.md
text: writing bug issue, follow template:
code: ## Bug Report  ### Description [Provide a clear and concise description of the bug. What behavior did you observe that you believe to be a bug?]  ### Reproducible Example [Include a minimal, complete, and verifiable example that demonstrates the bug. This could be a code snippet, dataset, or specific steps to reproduce the issue.]  ### Expected Behavior [Describe what you expected to happen when you encountered the bug.]  ### Actual Behavior [Explain what actually happened when you encountered the bug.]  ### Environment - R Version: [e.g., 4.5.1] - Package Version: [e.g., 1.2.3] - Operating System: [e.g., Windows 10, macOS 11.1]  ### Additional Context [Add any additional context, information, or examples that can help in understanding and reproducing the bug.]  ### Impact [Explain the impact or consequences of this bug. How does it affect the package's functionality or the user experience?]  ### Related Issues [Are there any related issues, pull requests, or discussions that are relevant to this bug report?]

title: ADaM Program Generation Through Declarative Specifications
text: illustrate mighty’s declarative paradigm, consider following example define derive variable COMPLSFL ADSL domain. column indicates whether subject’s end--study status “COMPLETED.” specification uses mighty.metadata format four main sections: id: ADaM domain name (ADSL) keys: Primary key variables dataset population: Defines SDTM domains form base population columns: array column definitions derivation methods COMPLSFL column points code component called der_complsfl.
code: id: ADSL  keys:   - USUBJID   - STUDYID  population:   base:     - domain: DM       depends: NA       filter: NA   global:     - filter: NA       depends: NA  columns:   - id: USUBJID    - id: STUDYID    - id: EOSSTT    - id: COMPLSFL     component:       id: der_complsfl

title: ADaM Program Generation Through Declarative Specifications
text: Code components can either validated standard components custom (un-validated) components. example der_complsfl standard component. can see case simply refer component name id field. Custom components need specified path location: Notice .R extension. Custom components simply R scripts adhere simple rules. See vignette("code_components") details. mighty matures, components pre-defined ready use, fewer components need defined user. Standard components provided mighty.standards, mighty.component provides utilities rendering standard custom components. See vignette("adam_specification") details ADaM Specification data model. now, let’s assume der_complsfl doesn’t exist write :
code: - id: COMPLSFL     component:       id: path/to/custom/component/der_complsfl.R #' @title Completion Status Flag Calculation #' #' @description This script adds a `COMPLSFL` column to the input data frame, #' indicating whether a subject's end-of-study status is marked as "COMPLETED". #' #' @type derivation #' #' @depends ADSL EOSSTT #' #' @outputs COMPLSFL #' @code  # Add a new column COMPLSFL based on the EOSSTT value ADSL <- ADSL |> dplyr::mutate(   COMPLSFL = dplyr::case_when(     EOSSTT == "COMPLETED" ~ "Y",  # Mark "Y" if EOSSTT is "COMPLETED"     TRUE                  ~ "N"   # Mark "N" otherwise   ) )

title: ADaM Program Generation Through Declarative Specifications
text: mighty processes three key inputs automatically generate ADaM programs: ADaM domain specifications - Define structure requirements analysis datasets Code components - Reusable building blocks referenced specifications Data availability context - Information SDTM/ADaM domains columns currently available package intelligently orchestrates execution order derivations, parameters, imputations, organizing operations structured, production-ready ADaM programs. Note: Code components can sourced mighty.standards (processed via mighty.component) defined custom user components, depending project requirements. See vignette("adam_specification") details ADaM specifications, vignette("mighty_config") study metadata configuration, vignette("connect_to_data") details source data retrieved rendered programs.

Package names, features, functionality may evolve ecosystem matures.

text: function orchestrates complete workflow generating ADaM (Analysis Data Model) programs ADaM specifications trial metadata. processes specifications, validates dependencies, creates action sequences, renders executable R code ADaM dataset creation.

title: Generate Complete Set of ADaM Programs — generate_adam_code
code: generate_adam_code(   adam_specifications,   path_connector_config,   check_cross_domain_adam_dependencies = TRUE,   data_context = NULL )

title: Generate Complete Set of ADaM Programs — generate_adam_code
text: adam_specifications Character string. Directory path containing ADaM specification YAML files (one per domain) optionally _mighty.yml framework configuration file external dataset definitions keys. path_connector_config Character string. File path connector configuration file (e.g., "_connector.yml"). path inserted exactly written generated programs (validation transformation performed). generated programs use path connect data execute - mighty read validate connector configuration file. Prefix !expr  embed R expression evaluated runtime generated program (e.g., '!expr ::("_connector.yml")'). check_cross_domain_adam_dependencies Logical. TRUE (default), validates dependencies across different ADaM domains. FALSE, validates dependencies within individual domains. data_context Optional list environment providing additional context available data sources executable program generation. NULL, programs considered potentially executable.

title: Generate Complete Set of ADaM Programs — generate_adam_code
text: named list containing complete ADaM program generation results: programs Named list complete R programs, one per ADaM domain. element character string containing fully executable R script. Programs include dependency-ordered actions ready execute independently. Names prefixed required execution order. program_sequence debugging . Data.table containing complete action sequence fully rendered R code programs. row represents single action action metadata. Actions ordered dependency requirements within domain. provides detailed view execution plan compilation complete programs. executable_programs Named list programs can executed currently available data sources (determined data_context). Structure identical programs includes code required input data available. data_context NULL, match programs. Used identify ADaM derivations can generated given current data availability. executable_program_sequence debugging . Data.table containing action sequence executable programs . Structure similar program_sequence filtered include actions code can executed available data. Provides visibility specific transformations run executing available programs. edges debugging . Data.table defining dependency graph actions. Contains columns parent_node node_id, representing directed edges parent actions must execute child actions. Edges created column dependencies (one action produces column another action consumes) row dependencies (explicit row-level operations). Includes synthetic edges connecting actions dependencies domain initialization actions. Self-referential edges removed. Used debugging action execution order dependency resolution. actions debugging . Data.table containing base action configurations code rendering program organization. row represents single action columns: node_id (unique action identifier), domain (ADaM domain name), code_id (reference code component NA), type (action type: col_copy, col_rename, col_mutate, col_echo, col_compute, row_compute, init_domain, filter_domain), outputs (list column character vectors showing columns produced), depend_cols (nested data.table column_name, domain, domain_type showing column dependencies), depend_rows (list node_ids action depends row operations), parameters (named list user-provided parameters). Reflects internal action data model execution ordering. Used debugging action setup dependency validation. rendered_components debugging . Named list code components successfully rendered using Mustache templates generation process. element contains rendered R code specific component (e.g., derivation functions). Component names correspond code_id values referenced action specifications. Used inspecting template parameters resolved debugging component rendering.

title: Generate Complete Set of ADaM Programs — generate_adam_code
text: function executes following workflow: Reads validates ADaM specifications trial metadata Sets initial action configurations dependency validation Adds domain initialization, filtering, data reading actions Creates dependency graph organizes actions execution order Adds data writing actions checks executable status Renders R code complete executable program sets Compiles individual actions complete, runnable programs generated program includes necessary data transformations, derivations, output operations creating specific ADaM dataset according provided specifications.
